ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers tea-based beverages; no dedicated node for bubble tea without milk. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for bubble tea beverage category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard takeaway cup of bubble tea without milk at less-sugar level, approximately 500 g, as commonly served at Singaporean bubble tea stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Bubble Tea (No Milk, Less Sugar, No Topping) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for bubble tea without milk, less sugar, no topping (cr_id D08010005). Calcium field is reported as -1 (data not available in source).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bubble tea without milk at less-sugar level. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogy to lightly sweetened tea beverages with low sugar concentration (~1.1 g/100 g). The input record cited GI 70 (high) from an 'Embedded GI Database' which is not an approved source and is inconsistent with the very low carbohydrate content (1.12 g/100 g). At 500 g serving size, available carbohydrate = 5.6 g; glycemic load = 55 × 5.6 / 100 = 3.08, rounded to 3.1. Estimation basis: University of Sydney GI Database analogous food category — sweetened tea beverages.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar syrup in a liquid form with negligible fibre and potassium
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is 0 mg/100 g, this beverage contains added sugar syrup which promotes insulin-driven sodium retention; individuals with hypertension should choose plain unsweetened tea or water instead of any sweetened beverage variant.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar syrup; sweetened beverage regardless of reduced sugar level
type2_diabetes.guidance: Even at the less-sugar customisation level, this beverage contains added sugar syrup and is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid all sweetened bubble tea variants and opt for zero-sugar or unsweetened tea to prevent postprandial glycaemic excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar in liquid form with no protective nutrients (no fibre, no omega-3, no potassium)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages including reduced-sugar variants is associated with elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit sweetened beverages and prefer plain unsweetened tea which provides polyphenol antioxidants.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar with no meaningful anti-inflammatory components
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This beverage provides no anti-inflammatory benefit (no omega-3s, no material polyphenols, no fibre); individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose plain unsweetened green or black tea to obtain polyphenol anti-inflammatory benefits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bubble tea without milk (less sugar, no topping) is a freshly prepared tea-based or fruit-infused beverage sold widely at Singaporean bubble tea stalls and chains. Unlike the standard milk tea variant, this version contains no dairy or plant-based milk, and is served at a reduced sugar level (typically 25–50% of the standard sugar quantity) with no customisable toppings such as tapioca pearls or jelly. The base is brewed black tea, green tea, or a fruit-flavoured tea, sweetened with a small amount of sugar syrup. The resulting beverage is very low in calories (approximately 4 kcal per 100 g), with negligible protein and fat (both confirmed zero by formulation), and a low carbohydrate content of 1.12 g per 100 g, all of which is sugar from the added syrup. Despite the low absolute sugar content per 100 g, this is still classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage because added sugar syrup is present. This classification has clinical significance: it renders the drink unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension, as liquid sugar has a rapid glycaemic impact and promotes insulin-driven sodium retention. The beverage is dairy-free, vegan, gluten-free, and halal-suitable. It provides no meaningful fibre, potassium, or protective micronutrients. For individuals seeking a healthier alternative, plain unsweetened tea provides polyphenol antioxidants without the glycaemic burden of added sugar.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D08010005), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean food records. The match is exact. GI is estimated by analogy from an approved GI source due to absence of a direct measurement for this specific beverage variant.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero: this is a tea-and-water-based beverage with no protein-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. fat_g is confirmed zero: no fat-containing ingredients are present in this milk-free, topping-free tea beverage; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same formulation logic. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. iron_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. sodium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source. calcium_mg is set to -1: HPB Singapore Foodo database does not report a calcium value for this specific beverage entry (input per_100g calcium = -1.0); detailed calcium data for this tea preparation is not available in the HPB database as of 2026. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this specific bubble tea preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI note: the input record cited GI 70 from 'Embedded GI Database' which is not an approved source under Project Veritas v2 policy. GI has been re-estimated at 55 (low) by analogy to lightly sweetened tea beverages using the University of Sydney GI Database as the reference framework, consistent with the very low carbohydrate content of 1.12 g/100 g. The 'keto' dietary tag from the input has been removed as this beverage contains added sugar and does not meet standard ketogenic dietary criteria. Occasion tags 'breakfast' and 'snack' are preserved in meal_context.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' as per G5 validation feedback.
Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' as per G5 validation feedback.
Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to align with glycemic_index 55 as per Q4 validation feedback (GI 55 is low, not medium).

Recalculated glycemic_load_per_serving based on GI 55 and 5.6g available carbohydrate (1.12g/100g * 500g serving) to 3.1 (55 * 5.6 / 100 = 3.08). The previous GL of 6.2 was based on a conservative estimate for liquid sugar, but for consistency with the GI calculation, the direct GL calculation is more appropriate.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
